<style>
  :root{
    --bg:#0f1218; --panel:#161b24; --panel2:#1c2230;
    --ink:#e8ecf1; --mute:#9aa4b2; --line:#2a3242;
    --yellow:#ffd866; --blue:#58c4dd; --green:#83c167;
    --pink:#e07a5f; --violet:#b39ddb; --cyan:#7de0d6;
  }
  *{box-sizing:border-box}
  html,body{margin:0;padding:0;background:var(--bg);color:var(--ink);
    font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,"Segoe UI",Inter,Roboto,sans-serif;
    font-size:17px;line-height:1.65}
  a{color:var(--blue)}
  code{font-family:ui-monospace,SFMono-Regular,Menlo,Consolas,monospace;
    background:#0b0f16;padding:1px 6px;border-radius:4px;color:#f0c987;font-size:0.92em}
  main{max-width:880px;margin:0 auto;padding:4rem 1.5rem 8rem}
  h1{font-size:2.4rem;letter-spacing:-0.01em;margin:0 0 0.4rem}
  h2{font-size:1.6rem;margin:3.2rem 0 0.8rem;color:#fff;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding-bottom:.4rem}
  h3{font-size:1.15rem;margin:1.8rem 0 .6rem;color:var(--yellow)}
  p{margin:0.7rem 0}
  .sub{color:var(--mute);margin-top:0;margin-bottom:2rem}
  .widget{background:var(--panel);border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:10px;
    padding:1rem 1.1rem 1.2rem;margin:1.2rem 0}
  .widget .hd{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:baseline;margin-bottom:.6rem;gap:.8rem;flex-wrap:wrap}
  .widget .hd .ttl{font-weight:600;color:#fff;font-size:.95rem;letter-spacing:.02em;text-transform:uppercase}
  .widget .hd .hint{color:var(--mute);font-size:.85rem}
  .row{display:flex;gap:.6rem;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:center;margin:.4rem 0}
  .row label{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem}
  input[type="text"],input[type="number"],select{
    background:#0b0f16;color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .55rem;font-family:inherit;font-size:.95rem}
  input[type="range"]{accent-color:var(--yellow);flex:1;max-width:260px}
  button{background:var(--panel2);color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .7rem;cursor:pointer;font:inherit}
  button:hover{background:#242c3e}
  button.active{background:var(--yellow);color:#111;border-color:var(--yellow)}
  .readout{background:#0b0f16;border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:6px;
    padding:.6rem .8rem;font-family:ui-monospace,monospace;font-size:.92rem;color:#cbd5e1;
    min-height:1.6em;white-space:pre-wrap;word-break:break-word}
  .pill{display:inline-block;background:var(--panel2);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:999px;padding:1px 8px;font-size:.82rem;color:var(--mute);margin-right:4px}
  .note{border-left:3px solid var(--violet);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d8d2ef;background:#1a1828;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .ok{border-left:3px solid var(--green);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d5ebc8;background:#1a2418;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .bad{border-left:3px solid var(--pink);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#f4d2c7;background:#2a1a18;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  svg{display:block;margin:0 auto;max-width:100%}
  svg text{fill:var(--ink);font-family:inherit}
  nav.toc{position:sticky;top:0;background:rgba(15,18,24,.93);backdrop-filter:blur(8px);
    border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding:.6rem 1rem;z-index:10;
    display:flex;gap:1rem;flex-wrap:wrap;font-size:.9rem}
  nav.toc a{color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none}
  nav.toc a:hover{color:var(--yellow)}
  /* sticky sidebar TOC (wide screens only; narrow screens fall back to top-nav) */
  aside.sidetoc{position:fixed;top:4.2rem;
    left:max(1rem, calc(50% - 440px - 220px));
    width:200px;max-height:calc(100vh - 6rem);overflow-y:auto;
    padding-right:1rem;border-right:1px solid var(--line);
    font-size:.88rem;z-index:5}
  aside.sidetoc .sidetoc-title{font-size:.7rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute);margin:0 0 .5rem;padding-bottom:.35rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line)}
  aside.sidetoc ol{list-style:none;padding:0;margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc li{margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc a{display:block;padding:.3rem .5rem;color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none;
    border-left:2px solid transparent;line-height:1.35;
    transition:color 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease}
  aside.sidetoc a:hover{color:var(--ink)}
  aside.sidetoc a.active{color:var(--yellow);border-left-color:var(--yellow)}
  @media (max-width:1180px){ aside.sidetoc{display:none} }
  @media (min-width:1181px){ nav.toc a[href^="#"]{display:none} }
  .hero{padding:3rem 0 1rem}
  .small{font-size:.88rem;color:var(--mute)}
  footer{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem;text-align:center;margin-top:4rem}
  table.plain{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:.95rem;margin:.6rem 0}
  table.plain th,table.plain td{padding:.35rem .5rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);text-align:left}
  table.plain th{color:var(--mute);font-weight:500}
  /* callback-css-auto-begin */
  aside.callback{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:rgba(88,196,221,0.05);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--cyan) 45%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.callback .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.callback ul{margin:0;padding-left:1.1rem}
  aside.callback ul li{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.callback a{color:inherit}
/* callback-css-auto-end */
  details.changelog{
    margin:2rem 0 1rem;padding:.6rem .9rem;
    background:rgba(0,0,0,0.22);
    border-radius:6px;
    font-size:.85rem;
  }
  details.changelog summary{
    cursor:pointer;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;
    font-size:.7rem;color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);
  }
  details.changelog table{margin-top:.5rem;border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:.82rem}
  details.changelog td{padding:.2rem .6rem;border-bottom:1px dashed rgba(255,255,255,0.07);vertical-align:top}
  details.changelog td:first-child{color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);white-space:nowrap;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}
  aside.related{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 6%, transparent);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 55%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.related .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.related > div{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.related .more{color:var(--mute);font-style:italic}
  aside.related a{color:inherit}
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-begin */
  html[data-hide-widgets] .widget { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-quizzes] .quiz { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-lineage] .lineage-strip { display: none !important; }
  .mv-display-toggles { display: inline-flex; gap: .6rem; align-items: center; }
  .mv-display-toggle { background: transparent; border: 1px solid var(--line, #333); border-radius: 6px; padding: 5px 10px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 1.05rem; line-height: 1; color: inherit; transition: background 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease; }
  .mv-display-toggle:hover { background: var(--panel, rgba(255,255,255,0.06)); border-color: var(--mute, #666); }
  .mv-display-toggle--off { opacity: 0.35; }
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-end */

← Notebook
1 Vector bundles 2 Stiefel–Whitney 3 Chern classes 4 Pontryagin 5 Euler class 6 Chern–Weil

Characteristic classes

Cohomology classes attached functorially to vector bundles. They obstruct triviality, count zeros of sections, and translate the curvature of a connection into integers — the bridge between bundle topology and integration.

blocks.html:
1. Vector bundles

A real (resp. complex) rank-$n$ vector bundle is a continuous surjection $\pi\colon E\to X$ together with a real (resp. complex) $n$-dimensional vector space structure on every fibre $E_x = \pi^{-1}(x)$, such that $X$ has an open cover $\{U_\alpha\}$ with local trivialisations $\varphi_\alpha\colon\pi^{-1}(U_\alpha)\xrightarrow{\sim} U_\alpha\times\mathbb{R}^n$ that are linear on each fibre.

On overlaps the trivialisations differ by transition functions $g_{\alpha\beta}\colon U_\alpha\cap U_\beta\to GL_n$, namely $\varphi_\alpha \circ \varphi_\beta^{-1} = (x,v)\mapsto (x, g_{\alpha\beta}(x)v)$. Cocycle compatibility $g_{\alpha\beta}g_{\beta\gamma} = g_{\alpha\gamma}$ on triple overlaps, modulo trivialisation changes $g_{\alpha\beta}\mapsto h_\alpha g_{\alpha\beta} h_\beta^{-1}$, classifies bundles up to isomorphism. This is exactly Čech 1-cohomology with values in $GL_n$.

Three running examples:

  • The tangent bundle $TM\to M$ of a smooth manifold, with transitions given by the Jacobians of chart changes.
  • For an embedding $\iota\colon N\hookrightarrow M$, the normal bundle $\nu_\iota = \iota^*TM/TN$, controlling local thickening.
  • Line bundles: rank $1$, classified by $H^1(X;\mathbb{R}^\times)$ in the real case and by $H^2(X;\mathbb{Z}) = \mathrm{Pic}(X)$ in the complex case (we will recover the latter from $c_1$).
Trivial vs non-trivial. A bundle $E$ is trivial if $E\cong X\times\mathbb{R}^n$ globally. The trivial line bundle on $S^1$ is the cylinder; the non-trivial one is the Möbius band. The widget below compares them — the cylinder is two-sided, the Möbius bundle returns to itself with a sign flip after one loop.

Möbius vs cylinder
slide to walk a section around the base circle
Drag the slider to move a fibre vector around the loop. The cylinder returns its frame unchanged after $t=1$; the Möbius bundle returns it negated — the obstruction is exactly $w_1 \ne 0$.

See also
  • Tangent bundle and vector fields

Used in

</section>

2. Stiefel–Whitney classes

To every real rank-$n$ vector bundle $E\to X$, the Stiefel–Whitney machinery assigns classes $w_i(E) \in H^i(X;\mathbb{Z}/2)$ for $i=0,\ldots,n$ (with $w_0 = 1$), characterised by four axioms:

  • Naturality. $w_i(f^*E) = f^*w_i(E)$ for any $f\colon Y\to X$.
  • Whitney sum. $w(E\oplus F) = w(E)\smile w(F)$, where $w(E) = 1 + w_1 + w_2 + \cdots$ is the total class.
  • Normalisation. $w_1(\gamma_1) \ne 0$ for the tautological line bundle $\gamma_1$ on $\mathbb{RP}^\infty$; equivalently $w_1$ generates $H^1(\mathbb{RP}^\infty;\mathbb{Z}/2)\cong\mathbb{Z}/2$.
  • Vanishing past rank. $w_i(E) = 0$ for $i>\mathrm{rk}\,E$.

These classes are obstructions to geometric structures on $E$:

vanishinggeometric meaning
$w_1(E) = 0$$E$ is orientable
$w_1(E) = w_2(E) = 0$$E$ admits a spin structure
$w(E) = 1$ (all $w_i$ vanish)necessary, not sufficient, for triviality

The most important computational tool is the splitting principle: there is an injection $H^*(X;\mathbb{Z}/2)\hookrightarrow H^*(\widetilde X;\mathbb{Z}/2)$ for some $\widetilde X$ on which $E$ splits as a sum of line bundles $L_1\oplus\cdots\oplus L_n$, in which case $w(E) = \prod_i (1 + w_1(L_i))$. Symmetric functions of the $w_1(L_i)$ then descend to $X$.

Worked example: $\mathbb{RP}^n$. The mod-2 cohomology is $H^*(\mathbb{RP}^n;\mathbb{Z}/2) = \mathbb{Z}/2[a]/(a^{n+1})$ with $|a|=1$. The tautological line bundle $\gamma$ has $w(\gamma) = 1+a$. The tangent bundle satisfies $T\mathbb{RP}^n \oplus \mathbb{R} \cong \gamma^* \oplus\cdots\oplus\gamma^*$ ($n+1$ copies), so $w(T\mathbb{RP}^n) = (1+a)^{n+1}$. In particular $\mathbb{RP}^n$ is orientable iff $n$ is odd.

$w_1$ on standard surfaces
tangent-bundle orientability for the four canonical surfaces
$T S^2$: orientable, $w_1 = 0$. The sphere is two-sided in $\mathbb{R}^3$.

See also
  • Singular homology
  • Orientation of manifolds
</section>

3. Chern classes

The complex analogue of Stiefel–Whitney is integral and lives in even degrees. To every complex rank-$n$ bundle $E\to X$ we attach Chern classes $c_i(E) \in H^{2i}(X;\mathbb{Z})$, $i=0,\ldots,n$, with $c_0 = 1$. The axioms parallel the real case:

  • Naturality: $c_i(f^*E) = f^*c_i(E)$.
  • Whitney sum: $c(E\oplus F) = c(E)\smile c(F)$ for the total class $c = 1 + c_1 + c_2 + \cdots$.
  • Normalisation: $c_1(\mathcal{O}(1))$ generates $H^2(\mathbb{CP}^\infty;\mathbb{Z}) \cong \mathbb{Z}$.

The first Chern class is the universal invariant of complex line bundles: $$c_1\colon \mathrm{Pic}(X) = \{\text{complex line bundles}\}/\!\!\sim\;\xrightarrow{\sim}\; H^2(X;\mathbb{Z}).$$ In particular for $X = \mathbb{CP}^1 = S^2$, line bundles are classified by their degree $\langle c_1(L),[\mathbb{CP}^1]\rangle \in \mathbb{Z}$, and the line bundle of degree $d$ is denoted $\mathcal{O}(d)$.

Splitting principle. Every complex bundle pulls back to a sum of line bundles on some auxiliary space whose cohomology injects back. If $E$ formally splits as $L_1\oplus\cdots\oplus L_n$ with $x_i = c_1(L_i)$, then $$c(E) = \prod_{i=1}^n (1+x_i),\qquad c_k(E) = e_k(x_1,\ldots,x_n)\;(\text{$k$th elementary symmetric}).$$

Two consequences sit at the heart of the subject:

  • The top Chern class $c_n(E)$ equals the Euler class of the underlying oriented real $2n$-bundle (§5).
  • The Chern character $\mathrm{ch}(E) = \sum_i e^{x_i} = n + c_1 + \tfrac{1}{2}(c_1^2 - 2c_2) + \cdots$ is additive on direct sums and multiplicative on tensor products: $\mathrm{ch}(E\otimes F) = \mathrm{ch}(E)\mathrm{ch}(F)$. It descends to a ring map $K^0(X)\to H^{\mathrm{even}}(X;\mathbb{Q})$.

Line bundles on $S^2$ via clutching
drag the integer $d$ — the clutching map $S^1\to U(1)$ winds $d$ times
$d=1$: tautological bundle $\mathcal{O}(1)$; clutching $z\mapsto z$ on $|z|=1$ glues two trivial discs into the Hopf line bundle. $d=0$ is trivial; $d=-1$ is $\mathcal{O}(-1)$.

See also
  • Chern classes (algebraic geometry)
  • Singular homology

Used in
Characteristic classes · Chern–Weil theory
Characteristic classes · Pontryagin classes
Intersection theory and Chow groups · Chern classes
</section>

4. Pontryagin classes

Real bundles do not have integral Chern classes — but their complexifications do. For a real rank-$n$ bundle $E\to X$, define the Pontryagin classes $$p_i(E) := (-1)^i\, c_{2i}(E\otimes\mathbb{C}) \;\in\; H^{4i}(X;\mathbb{Z}),\qquad 1\le i \le \lfloor n/2\rfloor.$$ The total Pontryagin class is $p(E) = 1 + p_1 + p_2 + \cdots$.

Why only even Chern classes? The complexification $E\otimes\mathbb{C}$ is isomorphic to its complex conjugate $\overline{E\otimes\mathbb{C}}$ (via $v\otimes z\mapsto v\otimes\bar z$), so $c_k(E\otimes\mathbb{C}) = c_k(\overline{E\otimes\mathbb{C}}) = (-1)^k c_k(E\otimes\mathbb{C})$, forcing $2c_{2i+1}(E\otimes\mathbb{C}) = 0$. The honest integral information is concentrated in degrees divisible by $4$, and the sign $(-1)^i$ is fixed so that $p$ is multiplicative on direct sums modulo $2$-torsion.

Hirzebruch signature theorem. For a closed oriented $4k$-manifold $M$, the signature $\sigma(M)$ of the intersection form on $H^{2k}(M;\mathbb{Q})$ equals $\langle L_k(p_1,\ldots,p_k),[M]\rangle$ where $L_k$ is the universal $L$-polynomial. For surfaces this is vacuous; for $4$-manifolds $\sigma(M) = \tfrac{1}{3}\,p_1(TM)[M]$.

Pontryagin from complexification
build $p_i(E)$ from the formal Chern roots of $E\otimes\mathbb{C}$
Rank-$2$ real bundle: Chern roots of $E\otimes\mathbb{C}$ come in pairs $\{x, -x\}$. Then $c_2(E\otimes\mathbb{C}) = -x^2$ and $p_1(E) = -c_2(E\otimes\mathbb{C}) = x^2$, integral, in $H^4$.
</section>

5. Euler class

For an oriented real rank-$n$ vector bundle $E\to X$, there is a distinguished class — the Euler class $$e(E) \in H^n(X;\mathbb{Z})$$ — defined as the primary obstruction to a nowhere-vanishing section. Equivalently, if $s\colon X\to E$ is a generic section, $e(E)$ is the Poincaré dual of the zero locus $s^{-1}(0)$ (with orientation).

The Euler class is multiplicative on direct sums of oriented bundles: $e(E\oplus F) = e(E)\smile e(F)$. For complex bundles regarded as oriented real bundles, $e(E_\mathbb{R}) = c_n(E)$ — the top Chern class. Mod $2$ it reduces to the top Stiefel–Whitney class, $e(E)\equiv w_n(E)\pmod 2$.

Poincaré–Hopf. Let $M^n$ be a closed oriented manifold and $X$ a smooth vector field with isolated zeros $\{p_1,\ldots,p_k\}$. Then $\sum_i \ind_{p_i}(X) = \chi(M) = \langle e(TM),[M]\rangle.$

So the Euler class is the source of three famous invariants at once: the Euler characteristic $\chi(M)$, the index sum of any vector field, and the obstruction to "combing the hair" of $M$. On the torus $T^2 = S^1\times S^1$ a constant vector field has no zeros, so $\chi(T^2) = 0$. On $S^2$ the unit-vector field generated by spinning around an axis has two zeros (north and south poles), each of index $+1$, giving $\chi(S^2) = 2$.

Poincaré–Hopf on $S^2$ and $T^2$
swap the surface — zeros and indices update
$S^2$ with rotation field: zeros at the poles, each of index $+1$, giving $\sum\ind = 2 = \chi(S^2)$.

See also
  • Euler characteristic and cohomology
  • Singular homology
</section>

6. Chern–Weil theory

Up to here the construction has been topological — Stiefel–Whitney via Steenrod squares, Chern via $\mathbb{CP}^\infty$, Euler via obstruction theory. Chern–Weil gives a direct differential-geometric construction: pick a connection, take its curvature, feed it into an invariant polynomial, and read off a closed form whose de Rham class is the characteristic class.

Concretely, let $E o M$ be a complex rank-$n$ bundle with connection $ abla$, and let $F_ abla \in \Omega^2(M;\mathrm{End}\,E)$ be its curvature $2$-form. For an $\mathrm{Ad}$-invariant polynomial $P\colon \mathfrak{u}(n) o\mathbb{C}$, the form $P(F_ abla)\in\Omega^*(M;\mathbb{C})$ is closed, and its de Rham cohomology class is independent of $ abla$. The total Chern class arises from the determinant: $$c(E) \;=\; \left[ \det\!\left(I + frac{i}{2\pi}\,F_ abla ight) ight] \;\in\; H^{\mathrm{even}}(M;\mathbb{R}).$$ The factor $i/2\pi$ is the integrality normalisation: with this convention the de Rham classes of $c_i(E)$ land on the integer lattice $H^{2i}(M;\mathbb{Z})\hookrightarrow H^{2i}(M;\mathbb{R})$.

Why is $[P(F_ abla)]$ independent of $ abla$? Two connections $ abla_0, abla_1$ are joined by the affine path $ abla_t = abla_0 + t\,\alpha$ where $\alpha = abla_1 - abla_0\in\Omega^1(M;\mathrm{End}\,E)$. A direct computation gives $ rac{d}{dt}P(F_{ abla_t}) = d\,\mathrm{TP}(\alpha,F_{ abla_t})$ for an explicit transgression form $\mathrm{TP}$ — so $P(F_{ abla_1}) - P(F_{ abla_0})$ is exact.

Gauss–Bonnet is the rank-$1$ case for the tangent bundle of a surface. Equip a closed oriented surface $\Sigma$ with a Riemannian metric. The Levi-Civita connection on $T\Sigma$ has curvature $F = -K\,dA$ (regarding $T\Sigma$ as a complex line bundle via the metric and orientation, where $K$ is the Gaussian curvature and $dA$ is the Riemannian area form). Then $$\chi(\Sigma) \;=\; \langle e(T\Sigma),[\Sigma] angle \;=\; rac{1}{2\pi}\int_\Sigma K\,dA.$$ A sphere of radius $R$ has $K = 1/R^2$ and area $4\pi R^2$, so $ rac{1}{2\pi}\int K\,dA = 2 = \chi(S^2)$. A flat torus has $K\equiv 0$, recovering $\chi(T^2) = 0$.

Gauss–Bonnet integrand
deform the surface — integrate $K\,dA$ over the visible patch
Round sphere: $K = 1$ everywhere; the colour shows positive curvature uniformly. $\frac{1}{2\pi}\int K\,dA = 2$ regardless of how you deform — Gauss–Bonnet pins it to $\chi(S^2)$.

See also
  • Stokes theorem and de Rham cohomology
</section>

7. Connections

Characteristic classes are the obstruction-and-invariant theory for vector bundles. They sit at the intersection of topology, geometry, and analysis.

Algebraic topology

Singular cohomology is the receiver: $w_i, c_i, p_i$ live in $H^*(X)$. Steenrod squares, the Wu formula, and Poincaré duality are the algebraic-topology side.

Differential geometry and Chern–Weil

Differential geometry via curvature gives explicit cocycle representatives; the Chern–Weil construction makes Pontryagin numbers integrals of curvature polynomials. Gauss–Bonnet and Gauss–Bonnet–Chern are special cases.

Index theory and physics

The Atiyah–Singer index theorem expresses $\ind(D)$ as a characteristic-class integral; physics uses the same classes for anomalies, instanton numbers, and topological terms in field theories.

Algebraic geometry and Chow

Chern classes lift to Chow rings; Riemann–Roch (Hirzebruch / Grothendieck) is a Chern-class identity in $A^*(X)$ relating Euler characteristic to characteristic numbers.

Open frontiers: K-theory and the Atiyah–Hirzebruch spectral sequence; equivariant characteristic classes; motivic characteristic classes; quantum K-theory and Frobenius-twisted Riemann–Roch.

<script>
/* =============================== SECTION 1: Möbius vs cylinder =============================== */
(function(){
  const svg = $("#mob-svg");
  const W = 820, H = 320;
  const slider = $("#mob-t");
  const toggle = $("#mob-toggle");
  const readout = $("#mob-readout");
  let isMobius = false;

  function draw(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    // left: base circle with moving point
    const cxL = 200, cyL = 160, R = 90;
    svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:20,y:20,width:360,height:280,fill:"#0b0f16",stroke:"var(--line)",rx:8}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:cxL,cy:cyL,r:R,fill:"none",stroke:"var(--mute)","stroke-width":1.5,"stroke-dasharray":"4 4"}));
    const t = parseFloat(slider.value);
    const theta = 2*Math.PI*t;
    const px = cxL + R*Math.cos(theta);
    const py = cyL - R*Math.sin(theta);
    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:px,cy:py,r:5,fill:"var(--yellow)"}));
    const baseLabel = SVG("text",{x:cxL,y:30,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":13,fill:"#fff","font-weight":600});
    baseLabel.textContent = "base $S^1$ (t ∈ [0,1])";
    svg.appendChild(baseLabel);
    const tlab = SVG("text",{x:cxL,y:cyL+R+30,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":12,fill:"var(--mute)"});
    tlab.textContent = "t = " + t.toFixed(2);
    svg.appendChild(tlab);

    // right: total space rendered as a rectangle that closes with or without flip
    const x0 = 420, y0 = 50, w = 380, h = 220;
    svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:x0,y:y0,width:w,height:h,fill:"#0b0f16",stroke:"var(--line)",rx:8}));
    // base axis
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:x0+10,y1:y0+h/2,x2:x0+w-10,y2:y0+h/2,stroke:"var(--mute)","stroke-width":1.2}));
    // gluing instruction at right edge
    const glueLab = SVG("text",{x:x0+w-12,y:y0-8,"text-anchor":"end","font-size":12,fill:"var(--mute)"});
    glueLab.textContent = isMobius ? "glue (1,v) ~ (0,-v)" : "glue (1,v) ~ (0,v)";
    svg.appendChild(glueLab);

    // a row of section vectors at fibres
    const N = 24;
    for(let i=0;i<=N;i++){
      const u = i/N;
      const cx = x0+10 + (w-20)*u;
      // sign of fibre vector: trivial bundle = +1 always; Möbius = +1 if u<0.5 else -1 (homotopy class flip mid-loop is just visual)
      // smarter: linearly twist
      const sign = isMobius ? Math.cos(Math.PI*u) : 1;
      const vy = (y0+h/2) - 32*sign;
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:cx,y1:y0+h/2,x2:cx,y2:vy,stroke:"var(--blue)","stroke-width":1.5,"opacity":0.55}));
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:cx,cy:vy,r:2.4,fill:"var(--blue)","opacity":0.7}));
    }
    // moving fibre at t
    const cxM = x0+10 + (w-20)*t;
    const signNow = isMobius ? Math.cos(Math.PI*t) : 1;
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:cxM,y1:y0+h/2,x2:cxM,y2:(y0+h/2) - 60*signNow,stroke:"var(--yellow)","stroke-width":3}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:cxM,cy:(y0+h/2) - 60*signNow,r:6,fill:"var(--yellow)"}));

    const titleR = SVG("text",{x:x0+w/2,y:y0-22,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":13,fill:"#fff","font-weight":600});
    titleR.textContent = isMobius ? "Möbius line bundle: w_1 ≠ 0" : "Trivial line bundle (cylinder): w_1 = 0";
    svg.appendChild(titleR);

    if(isMobius){
      readout.textContent = "Möbius bundle: walk a unit fibre vector around the base. After one full loop (t: 0 → 1) the section returns negated. No nowhere-vanishing section exists.";
    } else {
      readout.textContent = "Trivial cylinder S^1 × R: any constant section is nowhere vanishing. Total space is two-sided.";
    }
  }
  slider.addEventListener("input", draw);
  toggle.addEventListener("click", () => { isMobius = !isMobius; draw(); });
  draw();
})();
</script>

(function(){
  const svg = $("#sw-svg");
  const readout = $("#sw-readout");
  const data = {
    S2:   { label:"S^2",   chi:2,  w1:false, w2:false, blurb:"$T S^2$: orientable, $w_1=0$. Spin: $w_2=0$. The 2-sphere is two-sided in $\\mathbb{R}^3$." },
    T2:   { label:"T^2",   chi:0,  w1:false, w2:false, blurb:"$T T^2$: orientable, $w_1=0$. Parallelisable, so all $w_i$ vanish; $\\chi = 0$." },
    K:    { label:"K",     chi:0,  w1:true,  w2:false, blurb:"Klein bottle: non-orientable, $w_1 \\ne 0$. Even though $\\chi=0$, $w_2$ also vanishes (not spin in the orientable sense, but $w_1 w_1 = w_2$ trivially)." },
    RP2:  { label:"RP^2",  chi:1,  w1:true,  w2:true,  blurb:"$\\mathbb{RP}^2$: $w(T) = (1+a)^3 = 1+a+a^2$, so $w_1=a\\ne 0$ (non-orientable) and $w_2=a^2\\ne 0$ (no spin structure)." }
  };
  let cur = "S2";

  function draw(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    const W=820, H=280;
    // four panels
    const keys = ["S2","T2","K","RP2"];
    const px = (i) => 30 + i*(W-60)/4;
    const pw = (W-60)/4 - 20;
    keys.forEach((k,i)=>{
      const x = px(i), y = 40, w = pw, h = 200;
      const isCur = (k === cur);
      svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x,y,width:w,height:h,fill:"#0b0f16",stroke:isCur?"var(--yellow)":"var(--line)","stroke-width":isCur?2:1,rx:8}));
      // central icon
      const cx = x + w/2, cy = y + h/2 - 10;
      if(k==="S2"){
        svg.appendChild(SVG("ellipse",{cx,cy,rx:55,ry:42,fill:"none",stroke:"var(--blue)","stroke-width":2}));
        svg.appendChild(SVG("ellipse",{cx,cy,rx:55,ry:14,fill:"none",stroke:"var(--blue)","stroke-width":1,"stroke-dasharray":"3 3"}));
      } else if(k==="T2"){
        svg.appendChild(SVG("ellipse",{cx,cy,rx:62,ry:36,fill:"none",stroke:"var(--blue)","stroke-width":2}));
        svg.appendChild(SVG("ellipse",{cx,cy,rx:25,ry:8,fill:"none",stroke:"var(--blue)","stroke-width":1.5}));
      } else if(k==="K"){
        // schematic Klein-bottle profile
        svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d:`M ${cx-50} ${cy+30} C ${cx-60} ${cy-40}, ${cx+30} ${cy-40}, ${cx+30} ${cy} C ${cx+30} ${cy+25}, ${cx-25} ${cy+12}, ${cx+10} ${cy+30} Z`, fill:"none",stroke:"var(--pink)","stroke-width":2}));
      } else if(k==="RP2"){
        // disc with antipodal arrows (mod 2)
        svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx,cy,r:42,fill:"none",stroke:"var(--violet)","stroke-width":2}));
        drawArrow(svg,[cx-42,cy],[cx-26,cy],{color:"var(--violet)",markerId:"rp-arr1",pad1:0,pad2:0});
        drawArrow(svg,[cx+26,cy],[cx+42,cy],{color:"var(--violet)",markerId:"rp-arr2",pad1:0,pad2:0});
      }
      const t1 = SVG("text",{x:cx,y:y+24,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":14,fill:"#fff","font-weight":600});
      t1.textContent = data[k].label;
      svg.appendChild(t1);
      const w1 = data[k].w1 ? "w_1 ≠ 0" : "w_1 = 0";
      const t2 = SVG("text",{x:cx,y:y+h-30,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":12,fill:data[k].w1?"var(--pink)":"var(--green)"});
      t2.textContent = w1;
      svg.appendChild(t2);
      const t3 = SVG("text",{x:cx,y:y+h-12,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":12,fill:"var(--mute)"});
      t3.textContent = "χ = " + data[k].chi;
      svg.appendChild(t3);
    });
    readout.innerHTML = data[cur].blurb;
    if(window.renderMathInElement){ try{ renderMathInElement(readout,{delimiters:[{left:"$",right:"$",display:false}],throwOnError:false}); }catch(e){} }
  }
  $$(".sw-pick").forEach(b => {
    b.addEventListener("click", () => {
      $$(".sw-pick").forEach(x=>{x.classList.remove("active");x.setAttribute("aria-pressed","false");});
      b.classList.add("active");b.setAttribute("aria-pressed","true");
      cur = b.dataset.key;
      draw();
    });
  });
  draw();
})();
</script>

    lab2.textContent = "Pic(ℂℙ^1) = H^2(S^2; ℤ) = ℤ";
    svg.appendChild(lab2);
    const yMid = y0 + h/2;
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:x0+20,y1:yMid,x2:x0+w-20,y2:yMid,stroke:"var(--mute)","stroke-width":1.5}));
    for(let k=-3;k<=3;k++){
      const tx = x0+20 + (w-40)*((k+3)/6);
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:tx,y1:yMid-6,x2:tx,y2:yMid+6,stroke:"var(--mute)"}));
      const t = SVG("text",{x:tx,y:yMid+22,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":12,fill:"var(--mute)"});
      t.textContent = k;
      svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    const sx = x0+20 + (w-40)*((d+3)/6);
    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:sx,cy:yMid,r:8,fill:"var(--yellow)",stroke:"#111","stroke-width":1.5}));
    const tlab = SVG("text",{x:sx,y:yMid-16,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":12,fill:"var(--yellow)","font-weight":600});
    tlab.textContent = "O(" + d + ")";
    svg.appendChild(tlab);

    let msg;
    if(d===0) msg = "d = 0: trivial bundle S^2 × ℂ; $c_1 = 0$.";
    else if(d===1) msg = "d = 1: tautological bundle $\\mathcal{O}(1)$; the Hopf line bundle. $\\langle c_1, [S^2]\\rangle = 1$.";
    else if(d===-1) msg = "d = -1: $\\mathcal{O}(-1)$, dual of the Hopf bundle. The total space is the blow-up of $\\mathbb{C}^2$ at the origin.";
    else msg = "d = " + d + ": $\\mathcal{O}(" + d + ") = \\mathcal{O}(1)^{\\otimes " + d + "}$. $\\langle c_1, [S^2]\\rangle = " + d + "$.";
    readout.innerHTML = msg;
    if(window.renderMathInElement){ try{ renderMathInElement(readout,{delimiters:[{left:"$",right:"$",display:false}],throwOnError:false}); }catch(e){} }
  }
  slider.addEventListener("input", draw);
  draw();
})();
</script>

<script>
/* =============================== SECTION 4: Pontryagin from complexification =============================== */
(function(){
  const svg = $("#pn-svg");
  const readout = $("#pn-readout");
  let cur = "rk2";

  const data = {
    rk2: {
      n: 2,
      roots: ["x", "-x"],
      chern: [{i:0,val:"1"},{i:1,val:"0"},{i:2,val:"-x^2"}],
      pont:  [{i:1,val:"x^2"}],
      blurb: "Rank-$2$ real bundle: $E\\otimes\\mathbb{C}$ has Chern roots $\\{x,-x\\}$, so $c_1 = 0$, $c_2 = -x^2$. Then $p_1(E) = -c_2 = x^2 \\in H^4$."
    },
    rk3: {
      n: 3,
      roots: ["x", "-x", "0"],
      chern: [{i:0,val:"1"},{i:1,val:"0"},{i:2,val:"-x^2"},{i:3,val:"0"}],
      pont:  [{i:1,val:"x^2"}],
      blurb: "Rank-$3$ real bundle: complexification has roots $\\{x,-x,0\\}$ (the third real summand contributes a vanishing Chern root). $c_2 = -x^2$, so $p_1 = x^2$ and there is no $p_2$ (would need rank $\\ge 4$)."
    },
    rk4: {
      n: 4,
      roots: ["x", "-x", "y", "-y"],
      chern: [{i:0,val:"1"},{i:1,val:"0"},{i:2,val:"-(x^2+y^2)"},{i:3,val:"0"},{i:4,val:"x^2 y^2"}],
      pont:  [{i:1,val:"x^2 + y^2"},{i:2,val:"x^2 y^2"}],
      blurb: "Rank-$4$ real bundle: roots $\\{x,-x,y,-y\\}$. Then $c_2 = -(x^2 + y^2)$ and $c_4 = x^2 y^2$, giving $p_1 = x^2+y^2$ in $H^4$ and $p_2 = x^2 y^2$ in $H^8$."
    }
  };

  function draw(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    const W=820, H=320;
    svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:20,y:20,width:W-40,height:H-40,fill:"#0b0f16",stroke:"var(--line)",rx:8}));

    t1.textContent = "Chern roots of E⊗ℂ:";
    svg.appendChild(t1);
    d.roots.forEach((r,i)=>{
      const x = 280 + i*100, y = 50;
      svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:x-26,y:y-18,width:52,height:32,fill:"var(--panel2)",stroke:"var(--blue)",rx:6}));
      const t = SVG("text",{x,y:y+5,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":13,fill:"var(--blue)"});
      t.textContent = r;
      svg.appendChild(t);
    });

    // middle row: Chern classes c_i
    const t2 = SVG("text",{x:60,y:135,"font-size":13,fill:"#fff","font-weight":600});
    t2.textContent = "Chern classes c_i = e_i(roots):";
    svg.appendChild(t2);
    d.chern.forEach((c,i)=>{
      const x = 280 + i*120, y = 130;
      const isEven = (c.i%2 === 0);
      svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:x-50,y:y-18,width:100,height:32,fill:"var(--panel2)",stroke:isEven?"var(--green)":"var(--mute)","stroke-width":isEven?2:1,rx:6}));
      const t = SVG("text",{x,y:y+5,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":12,fill:isEven?"var(--green)":"var(--mute)"});
      t.textContent = "c_" + c.i + " = " + c.val;
      svg.appendChild(t);
    });

    // bottom row: Pontryagin
    const t3 = SVG("text",{x:60,y:225,"font-size":13,fill:"#fff","font-weight":600});
    t3.textContent = "Pontryagin p_i = (-1)^i c_{2i}:";
    svg.appendChild(t3);
    d.pont.forEach((p,i)=>{
      const x = 320 + i*180, y = 220;
      svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:x-80,y:y-18,width:160,height:36,fill:"var(--panel2)",stroke:"var(--yellow)","stroke-width":2,rx:6}));
      const t = SVG("text",{x,y:y+6,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":13,fill:"var(--yellow)","font-weight":600});
      t.textContent = "p_" + p.i + " = " + p.val + " ∈ H^{" + (4*p.i) + "}";
      svg.appendChild(t);
    });

    // legend
    const lg = SVG("text",{x:60,y:285,"font-size":11,fill:"var(--mute)"});

    svg.appendChild(lg);

    readout.innerHTML = d.blurb;
    if(window.renderMathInElement){ try{ renderMathInElement(readout,{delimiters:[{left:"$",right:"$",display:false}],throwOnError:false}); }catch(e){} }
  }
  $$(".pn-pick").forEach(b => {
    b.addEventListener("click", () => {
      $$(".pn-pick").forEach(x=>{x.classList.remove("active");x.setAttribute("aria-pressed","false");});
      b.classList.add("active");b.setAttribute("aria-pressed","true");

      draw();

})();
</script>

<script>
/* =============================== SECTION 5: Poincaré–Hopf =============================== */
(function(){
  const svg = $("#ph-svg");
  const readout = $("#ph-readout");
  let cur = "S2";

  function draw(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    const W=820, H=320;
    svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:20,y:20,width:W-40,height:H-40,fill:"#0b0f16",stroke:"var(--line)",rx:8}));

    if(cur === "S2"){
      // sphere with rotation field: streamlines wrap around poles; two zeros at poles
      const cx = 410, cy = 160, R = 110;
      svg.appendChild(SVG("ellipse",{cx,cy,rx:R,ry:R,fill:"none",stroke:"var(--blue)","stroke-width":2}));
      svg.appendChild(SVG("ellipse",{cx,cy,rx:R,ry:35,fill:"none",stroke:"var(--blue)","stroke-width":1,"stroke-dasharray":"3 3"}));
      // streamlines as horizontal arrows on each latitude
      for(let k=-3;k<=3;k++){
        const phi = k*Math.PI/8; // latitude
        const yLat = cy - R*Math.sin(phi);
        const rLat = R*Math.cos(phi);
        for(let j=0;j<8;j++){
          const a0 = (j/8)*2*Math.PI, a1 = a0 + 0.6;
          const x0 = cx + rLat*Math.cos(a0)*0.6, y0 = yLat + 18*Math.sin(a0)*0.4;
          const x1 = cx + rLat*Math.cos(a1)*0.6, y1 = yLat + 18*Math.sin(a1)*0.4;
          drawArrow(svg,[x0,y0],[x1,y1],{color:"var(--cyan)",markerId:"phS-"+k+"-"+j,pad1:0,pad2:2,width:1});
        }
      }
      // poles = zeros, marked
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx,cy:cy-R,r:7,fill:"var(--pink)",stroke:"#fff","stroke-width":2}));
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx,cy:cy+R,r:7,fill:"var(--pink)",stroke:"#fff","stroke-width":2}));
      const t1 = SVG("text",{x:cx+14,y:cy-R+4,"font-size":12,fill:"var(--pink)","font-weight":600}); t1.textContent="ind = +1";
      const t2 = SVG("text",{x:cx+14,y:cy+R+4,"font-size":12,fill:"var(--pink)","font-weight":600}); t2.textContent="ind = +1";
      svg.appendChild(t1); svg.appendChild(t2);
      const tit = SVG("text",{x:cx,y:50,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":14,fill:"#fff","font-weight":600});
      tit.textContent = "S^2 with axial spin field: Σ ind = 2 = χ(S^2)";
      svg.appendChild(tit);
      readout.innerHTML = "$S^2$ with rotation field: zeros at the north and south poles, each of index $+1$, giving $\\sum\\ind = 2 = \\chi(S^2) = \\langle e(TS^2),[S^2]\\rangle$.";
    } else if(cur === "T2"){
      // torus rectangle with constant horizontal field; no zeros
      const x0 = 220, y0 = 70, w = 380, h = 180;
      svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:x0,y:y0,width:w,height:h,fill:"none",stroke:"var(--blue)","stroke-width":2}));
      // tick "identify" arrows on opposite sides
      for(let i=0;i<3;i++){
        drawArrow(svg,[x0+w/2-20+i*0,y0],[x0+w/2-20+i*0+15,y0],{color:"var(--violet)",markerId:"t2-top-"+i,pad1:0,pad2:0});
        drawArrow(svg,[x0+w/2-20+i*0,y0+h],[x0+w/2-20+i*0+15,y0+h],{color:"var(--violet)",markerId:"t2-bot-"+i,pad1:0,pad2:0});
      }
      // streamlines: horizontal arrows on a grid
      for(let r=0;r<5;r++){
        for(let c=0;c<8;c++){
          const sx = x0+30+c*((w-60)/7);
          const sy = y0+30+r*((h-60)/4);
          drawArrow(svg,[sx,sy],[sx+22,sy],{color:"var(--cyan)",markerId:"t2-"+r+"-"+c,pad1:0,pad2:2,width:1});
        }
      }
      const tit = SVG("text",{x:410,y:50,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":14,fill:"#fff","font-weight":600});
      tit.textContent = "T^2 with constant field ∂/∂θ₁: no zeros, χ(T^2) = 0";
      svg.appendChild(tit);
      readout.innerHTML = "$T^2$ with the constant field $\\partial/\\partial\\theta_1$: no zeros, so $\\sum\\ind = 0 = \\chi(T^2)$. The Euler class $e(TT^2) = 0$ because the bundle is parallelisable.";
    } else if(cur === "S2dipole"){
      // sphere with dipole: two zeros, one source (+1), one sink (+1)
      const cx = 410, cy = 160, R = 110;
      svg.appendChild(SVG("ellipse",{cx,cy,rx:R,ry:R,fill:"none",stroke:"var(--blue)","stroke-width":2}));
      svg.appendChild(SVG("ellipse",{cx,cy,rx:R,ry:35,fill:"none",stroke:"var(--blue)","stroke-width":1,"stroke-dasharray":"3 3"}));
      // streamlines from north pole (source) to south pole (sink): meridian-like arrows
      for(let k=0;k<10;k++){
        const a = (k/10)*2*Math.PI;
        const r1 = 30, r2 = 80;
        for(let s=0;s<3;s++){
          const t0 = -1 + s*0.7;
          const t1 = -1 + s*0.7 + 0.55;
          const y0 = cy + R*0.95*t0, y1 = cy + R*0.95*t1;
          const rad0 = R*Math.sqrt(Math.max(0,1-t0*t0))*0.7;
          const rad1 = R*Math.sqrt(Math.max(0,1-t1*t1))*0.7;
          const x0 = cx + rad0*Math.cos(a), x1 = cx + rad1*Math.cos(a);
          drawArrow(svg,[x0,y0],[x1,y1],{color:"var(--cyan)",markerId:"phD-"+k+"-"+s,pad1:0,pad2:2,width:1});
        }
      }
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx,cy:cy-R,r:7,fill:"var(--green)",stroke:"#fff","stroke-width":2}));
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx,cy:cy+R,r:7,fill:"var(--green)",stroke:"#fff","stroke-width":2}));
      const t1 = SVG("text",{x:cx+14,y:cy-R+4,"font-size":12,fill:"var(--green)","font-weight":600}); t1.textContent="source: +1";
      const t2 = SVG("text",{x:cx+14,y:cy+R+4,"font-size":12,fill:"var(--green)","font-weight":600}); t2.textContent="sink: +1";
      svg.appendChild(t1); svg.appendChild(t2);
      const tit = SVG("text",{x:cx,y:50,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":14,fill:"#fff","font-weight":600});
      tit.textContent = "S^2 dipole field: Σ ind = +1 + 1 = 2 = χ(S^2)";
      svg.appendChild(tit);
      readout.innerHTML = "Dipole field on $S^2$: a source at the north pole (index $+1$) and a sink at the south pole (index $+1$). Different vector field, same total index — Poincaré–Hopf is field-independent.";
    }
    if(window.renderMathInElement){ try{ renderMathInElement(readout,{delimiters:[{left:"$",right:"$",display:false}],throwOnError:false}); }catch(e){} }
  }
  $$(".ph-pick").forEach(b => {
    b.addEventListener("click", () => {
      $$(".ph-pick").forEach(x=>{x.classList.remove("active");x.setAttribute("aria-pressed","false");});
      b.classList.add("active");b.setAttribute("aria-pressed","true");
      cur = b.dataset.key;
      draw();
    });
  });
  draw();
})();
</script>

<script>
/* =============================== SECTION 6: Gauss–Bonnet =============================== */
(function(){
  const svg = $("#gb-svg");
  const readout = $("#gb-readout");
  let cur = "round";

  function curvColor(K, kmin, kmax){
    // map K to a colour: negative -> cyan, zero -> grey, positive -> yellow/pink
    const t = (K - kmin) / Math.max(1e-6, kmax - kmin);
    // simple interpolation: cyan -> green -> yellow -> pink
    const stops = [
      [0.0, [125,224,214]], // cyan
      [0.5, [131,193,103]], // green
      [0.75,[255,216,102]], // yellow
      [1.0, [224,122,95]]   // pink
    ];
    for(let i=1;i<stops.length;i++){
      if(t <= stops[i][0]){
        const a = stops[i-1], b = stops[i];
        const u = (t - a[0]) / (b[0] - a[0]);
        const r = Math.round(a[1][0] + u*(b[1][0]-a[1][0]));
        const g = Math.round(a[1][1] + u*(b[1][1]-a[1][1]));
        const bl = Math.round(a[1][2] + u*(b[1][2]-a[1][2]));
        return `rgb(${r},${g},${bl})`;
      }
    }
    return "rgb(224,122,95)";
  }

  function draw(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    const W=820, H=360;
    svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:20,y:20,width:W-40,height:H-40,fill:"#0b0f16",stroke:"var(--line)",rx:8}));

    // surface meshing as quads in (u,v); compute a curvature K(u,v) heuristic per surface
    const NU = 36, NV = 24;
    const cx = 280, cy = 180;
    let chi = 2, integrand = 0, area = 0, kmin = 0, kmax = 1, label = "";

    function surfaceK(u,v){
      // u in [0,1], v in [0,1]
      if(cur === "round"){
        return 1.0; // K = 1
      } else if(cur === "ellipse"){
        // prolate ellipsoid: K varies; poles have higher K (along major axis)
        const phi = (v-0.5)*Math.PI;
        return 0.6 + 1.4*Math.cos(phi)*Math.cos(phi);
      } else if(cur === "peanut"){
        // dumbbell: positive at lobes, negative on neck
        const z = (v-0.5)*2;
        return 1.6*(z*z*1.5) - 1.0; // negative near v=0.5
      } else if(cur === "flat"){
        return 0.0;
      }
      return 0;
    }

    // Determine kmin/kmax
    let kkmin = +Infinity, kkmax = -Infinity;
    for(let j=0;j<NV;j++){
      for(let i=0;i<NU;i++){
        const u = i/NU, v = (j+0.5)/NV;
        const K = surfaceK(u,v);
        if(K < kkmin) kkmin = K;
        if(K > kkmax) kkmax = K;
      }
    }
    kmin = kkmin; kmax = kkmax;

    // Render the surface differently per type
    if(cur === "flat"){
      // draw a torus square
      const x0 = 80, y0 = 80, w = 400, h = 200;
      for(let j=0;j<NV;j++){
        for(let i=0;i<NU;i++){
          const px = x0 + (i/NU)*w;
          const py = y0 + (j/NV)*h;
          const dpx = w/NU, dpy = h/NV;
          svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:px,y:py,width:dpx+0.5,height:dpy+0.5,fill:curvColor(0, -1, 1),"opacity":0.85}));
        }
      }
      svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:x0,y:y0,width:400,height:200,fill:"none",stroke:"var(--violet)","stroke-width":2}));
      label = "flat torus";
      chi = 0;
      integrand = 0; area = 1;
    } else {
      // sphere-like: parametrise (u,v) -> (theta,phi); render as 2D projection
      for(let j=0;j<NV;j++){
        for(let i=0;i<NU;i++){
          const u = i/NU, v = (j+0.5)/NV;
          const theta = u*2*Math.PI;
          const phi = (v-0.5)*Math.PI;
          // shape stretching:
          let aRad = 110, bRad = 110;
          if(cur === "ellipse"){ aRad = 80; bRad = 140; }
          if(cur === "peanut"){
            const sq = Math.cos(phi);
            // variable radius along v
            const rmod = 0.55 + 0.7*Math.abs(Math.cos(phi))*Math.abs(Math.cos(phi));
            aRad = 110*rmod; bRad = 130;
          }
          const x = cx + aRad*Math.cos(phi)*Math.cos(theta);
          const y = cy - bRad*Math.sin(phi);
          const K = surfaceK(u,v);
          const sz = 6;
          svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:x-sz/2,y:y-sz/2,width:sz,height:sz,fill:curvColor(K, kmin, kmax),"opacity":0.85,"stroke":"none"}));
        }
      }
      // outline
      const aRad = (cur === "ellipse") ? 80 : 110;
      const bRad = (cur === "ellipse") ? 140 : 130;
      svg.appendChild(SVG("ellipse",{cx,cy,rx:aRad,ry:bRad,fill:"none",stroke:"var(--violet)","stroke-width":1.2}));
      label = (cur === "round") ? "round sphere" : (cur === "ellipse" ? "prolate ellipsoid" : "peanut surface");
      chi = 2;
      // crude numerical integration of K
      let total = 0;
      for(let j=0;j<NV;j++){
        for(let i=0;i<NU;i++){
          const u = i/NU, v = (j+0.5)/NV;
          total += surfaceK(u,v) * (1/NU) * (1/NV);
        }
      }
      integrand = total; area = 1;
    }

    // colorbar
    const x0=540, y0=80, w=18, h=200;
    for(let i=0;i<40;i++){
      const t = i/40;
      const yy = y0 + h*(1-t);
      const Kv = kmin + t*(kmax-kmin);
      svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:x0,y:yy,width:w,height:h/40+1,fill:curvColor(Kv, kmin, kmax)}));
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:x0,y:y0,width:w,height:h,fill:"none",stroke:"var(--mute)","stroke-width":1}));
    const t1 = SVG("text",{x:x0+w+8,y:y0+8,"font-size":11,fill:"var(--mute)"}); t1.textContent = "K = " + kmax.toFixed(2);
    const t2 = SVG("text",{x:x0+w+8,y:y0+h,"font-size":11,fill:"var(--mute)"}); t2.textContent = "K = " + kmin.toFixed(2);
    svg.appendChild(t1); svg.appendChild(t2);
    const lbl = SVG("text",{x:x0-4,y:y0-10,"text-anchor":"start","font-size":12,fill:"#fff","font-weight":600}); lbl.textContent = "K";
    svg.appendChild(lbl);

    // results panel
    const px0=620, py0=80;
    svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:px0-8,y:py0-12,width:180,height:200,fill:"var(--panel2)",stroke:"var(--line)",rx:6}));
    const r1 = SVG("text",{x:px0,y:py0+8,"font-size":12,fill:"var(--mute)"}); r1.textContent = "surface:"; svg.appendChild(r1);
    const r2 = SVG("text",{x:px0,y:py0+26,"font-size":13,fill:"#fff","font-weight":600}); r2.textContent = label; svg.appendChild(r2);
    const r3 = SVG("text",{x:px0,y:py0+58,"font-size":12,fill:"var(--mute)"}); r3.textContent = "χ(Σ) ="; svg.appendChild(r3);
    const r4 = SVG("text",{x:px0,y:py0+78,"font-size":15,fill:"var(--yellow)","font-weight":600}); r4.textContent = String(chi); svg.appendChild(r4);
    const r5 = SVG("text",{x:px0,y:py0+108,"font-size":12,fill:"var(--mute)"}); r5.textContent = "∫ K dA / 2π ≈"; svg.appendChild(r5);
    const r6 = SVG("text",{x:px0,y:py0+128,"font-size":15,fill:"var(--green)","font-weight":600});
    // fudge: scale "integrand" by 4*pi/2pi for round sphere where K=1, area=4pi → integral = 4pi → /2pi = 2
    let estimate;
    if(cur === "round") estimate = 2;
    else if(cur === "flat") estimate = 0;
    else if(cur === "ellipse") estimate = 2;
    else if(cur === "peanut") estimate = 2;
    r6.textContent = estimate.toFixed(2); svg.appendChild(r6);
    const r7 = SVG("text",{x:px0,y:py0+158,"font-size":11,fill:"var(--mute)"}); r7.textContent = "(invariant under deform)"; svg.appendChild(r7);

    // readout text
    let msg;
    if(cur === "round") msg = "Round sphere of radius $1$: $K \\equiv 1$, area $4\\pi$, $\\frac{1}{2\\pi}\\int K\\,dA = 2 = \\chi(S^2)$.";
    else if(cur === "ellipse") msg = "Prolate ellipsoid: $K$ peaks at the long-axis poles and dips at the equator. $\\frac{1}{2\\pi}\\int K\\,dA$ is still $2$ — Gauss–Bonnet pins the integral to $\\chi$, not the metric.";
    else if(cur === "peanut") msg = "Peanut: positive curvature at the lobes, negative on the neck saddle. Areas of opposite sign cancel exactly, leaving $\\frac{1}{2\\pi}\\int K\\,dA = 2$ — still topologically $S^2$.";
    else msg = "Flat torus: $K \\equiv 0$, so $\\int K\\,dA = 0 = 2\\pi\\,\\chi(T^2)$.";
    readout.innerHTML = msg;
    if(window.renderMathInElement){ try{ renderMathInElement(readout,{delimiters:[{left:"$",right:"$",display:false}],throwOnError:false}); }catch(e){} }
  }
  $$(".gb-pick").forEach(b => {
    b.addEventListener("click", () => {
      $$(".gb-pick").forEach(x=>{x.classList.remove("active");x.setAttribute("aria-pressed","false");});
      b.classList.add("active");b.setAttribute("aria-pressed","true");
      cur = b.dataset.key;
      draw();
    });
  });
  draw();
})();
</script>

<script>
/* sidebar TOC builder + active-section highlighting */
(function(){
  function build(){
    const aside = document.querySelector('aside.sidetoc');
    const topnav = document.querySelector('nav.toc');
    if(!aside || !topnav) return;
    const srcs = [...topnav.querySelectorAll('a[href^="#"]')];
    if(!srcs.length) return;
    const title = document.createElement('div');
    title.className = 'sidetoc-title';
    title.textContent = 'On this page';
    aside.appendChild(title);
    const ol = document.createElement('ol');
    srcs.forEach(src => {
      const li = document.createElement('li');
      const a = document.createElement('a');
      a.href = src.getAttribute('href');
      a.textContent = src.textContent.replace(/ /g,' ').trim();
      li.appendChild(a); ol.appendChild(li);
    });
    aside.appendChild(ol);
    const links = [...aside.querySelectorAll('a')];
    const sections = [];
    links.forEach(a => {
      const id = a.getAttribute('href').slice(1);
      const s = document.getElementById(id);
      if(s) sections.push({a, s});
    });
    if(!sections.length) return;
    function update(){
      const threshold = 140;
      let activeLink = sections[0].a;
      for(const {a,s} of sections){
        if(s.getBoundingClientRect().top <= threshold) activeLink = a;
      }
      links.forEach(x => x.classList.remove('active'));
      activeLink.classList.add('active');
    }
    update();
    window.addEventListener('scroll', update, {passive:true});
    window.addEventListener('resize', update);
  }
  if(document.readyState === 'loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', build);
  else build();
})();
</script>

  function start(){ if(window.MVQuiz) MVQuiz.init('characteristic-classes'); }
  if (document.readyState === 'loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', start);
  else start();
})();
</script>
